title: acer velutinum var. vanvolxemii
intro: Acer velutinum var. vanvolxemii, also known as the Van Volxem's downy maple or the downy maple, is a variety of maple tree native to Europe. The plant contains a compound called salicin, which has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including reducing pain and inflammation. The health benefits of Acer velutinum var. vanvolxemii include relief from colds, fever, and rheumatic pain, as well as aiding in digestion. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea by infusing the leaves in hot water, or used to make a salve by mixing the leaves with oil.
